name: 2023-12-ethereumcreditguild
note: The Ethereum Credit Guild — a trust-minimized credit/lending protocol with gauge-voted lending terms (LendingTerm), a Dutch-auction liquidation (AuctionHouse), a SimplePSM, surplus/loss accounting (ProfitManager), a rebasing CREDIT token (ERC20RebaseDistributor) and a GUILD gauge-voting token. Official C4 report: 4 High / 25 Medium. This manifest pins a conservative, high-confidence subset of those findings, each verified to a real (contract, function, file, line) in src at commit 2facb8f. Most are protocol-specific gauge/accounting/auction-lifecycle bugs (out-of-class for Sluice's modeled detector set); only the sequencer-uptime, rounding, and unbounded-loop findings are marked in_class. Mediums whose published locus could not be pinned to a single verified mechanism in source (M-05, M-08, M-09, M-10, M-11, M-15, M-17, M-18, M-24) were dropped rather than guessed. IDs carry the report's H-/M- number.

known_findings.summary: claimGaugeRewards initializes a first-time staker's reward baseline with `if (_userGaugeProfitIndex == 0) { _userGaugeProfitIndex = 1e18; }` (L424-426) rather than snapshotting the gauge's *current* gaugeProfitIndex at the time the user's weight is incremented. A user who increments gauge weight (GuildToken._incrementGaugeWeight -> claimGaugeRewards) after profit already accrued is credited the full `deltaIndex = _gaugeProfitIndex - 1e18` (L427-429), receiving rewards that accrued before they staked, with no waiting period (H-01). Protocol-specific reward-index accounting bug, no modeled detector.

transfer() (and transferFrom L646) snapshot `rebasingStateTo = rebasingState[to]` into memory at the top (L560), then update the `from` shares, then recompute the `to` shares from the STALE in-memory `rebasingStateTo.nShares` and a freshly re-read `rawToBalanceAfter = ERC20.balanceOf(to)` (L605-623). When `from == to` (self-transfer) and the address is rebasing, the sender-side burn of shares is not reflected in the stale `to` snapshot, so shares are double-counted and an attacker mints arbitrary balance / steals all distributed rebase rewards (H-02). Protocol-specific stale-snapshot share-accounting bug, no modeled detector.

When bad debt is realized the ProfitManager marks the creditMultiplier DOWN. onBid recomputes a loan's principal live from the current multiplier: `principal = (borrowAmount * loans[loanId].borrowCreditMultiplier) / creditMultiplier` (L754-755), and getLoanDebt does the same (L230). A drop in creditMultiplier during concurrent auctions inflates every other called loan's principal relative to its frozen callDebt, so settling one bad-debt auction forces additional loans in auction to also settle as bad debt — a cascade (H-03). Protocol-specific credit-multiplier mark-down accounting, no modeled detector.

getRewards evaluates the slash condition `if (lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss)) slashed = true;` (L229) BEFORE `userStake = _stakes[user][term]` is loaded (L234). On the uninitialized stack `userStake.lastGaugeLoss == 0`, so for any term that ever had a loss (lastGaugeLoss != 0) a brand-new staker is flagged slashed and immediately wiped (L274-284) on their first interaction (H-04). Protocol-specific uninitialized-memory ordering bug, no modeled detector.

getBidDetail derives the Dutch-auction price purely from `block.timestamp - startTime` against midPoint/auctionDuration (L134-160) with no L2 sequencer-uptime check; if the sequencer was down, on restart the auction has effectively skipped ahead (offering full collateral for near-zero CREDIT, or vice-versa), so auctions fail or execute at bad prices (M-01). Mapped to Sluice's OracleStaleness/SequencerUptime family (in_class).

RateLimitedMinter.mint is gated by `whenNotPaused` (L49-52). GUILD reward minting in SurplusGuildMinter (and credit reward forwarding) flows through this mint; when the protocol is paused, users staking via SurplusGuildMinter cannot get rewards minted and therefore cannot complete unstake/withdraw flows that depend on mint, locking funds for the pause duration (M-02). Protocol-specific pause-coupling logic, no modeled detector.

totalBorrowedCredit returns `CreditToken(credit).targetTotalSupply() - SimplePSM(psm).redeemableCredit()` (L172-176). redeemableCredit = getMintAmountOut(pegTokenBalance) scales with 1/creditMultiplier, so after a creditMultiplier change redeemableCredit can exceed targetTotalSupply and the subtraction underflows-reverts; because debtCeiling() calls totalBorrowedCredit, gauge weight decrements / borrows revert, breaking gauges (M-03). Protocol-specific accounting-underflow, no modeled detector.

On a loss beyond the surplus buffer, notifyPnL recomputes `newCreditMultiplier = (creditMultiplier * (creditTotalSupply - loss)) / creditTotalSupply` (L332-333). A large user can drive `loss` close to or beyond `creditTotalSupply` (or otherwise distort the ratio), collapsing the creditMultiplier toward zero in one event and breaking the system-wide PnL/peg accounting intentionally or unintentionally (M-04). Protocol-specific PnL-accounting bug, no modeled detector.

offboard() only requires `canOffboard[term]` (L154) and never resets it; the flag is cleared only in cleanup() (L197). Once a poll reaches quorum and canOffboard[term] is set true (supportOffboard L138-140), the flag (and the persisted poll weight) can be re-used to re-offboard a term that governance re-onboarded, bypassing a fresh DAO vote (M-06). Protocol-specific flag-lifecycle logic, no modeled detector.

_call() only permits calling a loan if `isDeprecatedGauge(term) || partialRepayDelayPassed(loanId)` (L652-656). A loan whose debt grows past maxDebtPerCollateralToken (undercollateralized) but whose term is neither deprecated nor has a missed periodic partial-repay cannot be called/liquidated, so the position sits creating bad debt with no liquidation path (M-07). Protocol-specific missing-liquidation-trigger, no modeled detector.

distribute() resets the reward interpolation window to `endTimestamp = block.timestamp + DISTRIBUTION_PERIOD` (30 days) on EVERY call (L364-384), updating __rebasingSharePrice/__unmintedRebaseRewards targets. Anyone can call distribute() with a tiny amount repeatedly to perpetually push the targetTimestamp forward, prolonging the time over which already-distributed rewards become realized to holders (M-12). Protocol-specific interpolation-reset logic, no modeled detector.

debtCeiling reads `creditMinterBuffer = RateLimitedMinter(refs.creditMinter).buffer()` (L282-283) and uses it as a min() cap on the returned ceiling (L290-291, L324-326). The creditMinter buffer is a global rate-limit shared across all terms, not a per-term capacity, so folding it into this term's debt ceiling mis-states the term's true borrowable amount (M-13). Protocol-specific buffer-misuse accounting, no modeled detector.

_partialRepay requires `principalRepaid != 0 && interestRepaid != 0` (L519-522). For a loan with 0 interest accrued (e.g. interestRate 0, or repaid in the same conditions where interest rounds to 0), interestRepaid is 0 and the call always reverts, so a borrower under a term with periodic partial-repay obligations cannot make the required partial repayment and the loan becomes callable (M-14). Protocol-specific conflicting-require logic, no modeled detector.

bid() settles a Dutch auction whose price is a pure function of elapsed blocks (getBidDetail L118). A bidder can use block stuffing and/or an ERC-777 receive/transfer hook (collateral push in LendingTerm.onBid) that reverts to grief competing bids and drag the auction into the cheaper phase, winning collateral below fair value (M-16). Protocol-specific auction-griefing logic, no modeled detector.

_decrementGaugeWeight only blocks an unstake when `issuance > debtCeiling(-int256(weight))` (L224-231); debtCeiling applies a gaugeWeightTolerance of 1.2e18 (120%) on top of the gauge weight (LendingTerm.debtCeiling L305). Because of that tolerance the computed post-decrement ceiling stays above issuance even after removing most of the weight, so a holder can unstake well over 90% of GUILD voting for a gauge that is using its full debt allocation (M-19). Protocol-specific tolerance-accounting bug, no modeled detector.

proposeOffboard requires `block.number > lastPollBlock[term] + POLL_DURATION_BLOCKS` (~7 days, L94-97), so a new offboard poll for a term cannot be opened within 7 days of the previous one. If a term needs to be offboarded twice in a 7-day window (e.g. re-onboarded then needs re-offboarding) the second poll cannot start, leaving bad debt to accrue (M-20). Protocol-specific poll-cooldown logic, no modeled detector.

SimplePSM.mint calls `CreditToken(credit).mint(to, amountOut)` directly (L110), and redeem burns directly (L140), bypassing the RateLimitedMinter that gates all other CREDIT issuance. CREDIT can therefore be minted/redeemed through the PSM without consuming the governance rate-limit buffer, weakening the issuance-velocity control that protects against governance/oracle attacks (M-21). Protocol-specific rate-limit-bypass logic, no modeled detector.

The borrow() debt-ceiling check uses `calculateGaugeAllocation(this, totalBorrowedCredit + borrowAmount) * gaugeWeightTolerance / 1e18` (L383-387), while the public debtCeiling() view computes the ceiling through a different toleratedGaugeWeight / otherGaugesWeight formula (L305-330). The two formulas can disagree, so debtCeiling() reports a value inconsistent with what borrow() actually enforces (M-22). Protocol-specific formula-inconsistency accounting bug, no modeled detector.

Share<->balance conversions round down in _balance2shares/_shares2balance (L256, L266-273). In _mint the realized reward `mintAmount = rebasedBalance - rawBalance` (L542) and the analogous subtractions in transfer/_burn can underflow when rounding makes the recomputed rebasedBalance momentarily less than the raw balance, reverting transfers and mints for affected balances (M-23). Genuine rounding-direction / precision-loss bug; mapped to Sluice's RoundingDirection/PrecisionLoss family (in_class).

The unbounded loop of M-25 lives in ProfitManager.claimRewards (L443, `for i < gauges.length` over the full per-user gauge list fetched from GuildToken.userGauges, each iteration doing reward accounting + a CREDIT transfer); it is reached cross-contract from SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards (L239, `ProfitManager(profitManager).claimRewards(address(this))`). As positions accumulate across many gauges/terms the loop can exceed the block gas limit, DoSing the claim path (M-25). Pinned to the TRUE mechanism locus (the loop in claimRewards) rather than the cross-contract entry getRewards, per the pin-to-real-mechanism rule. Mapped to Sluice's UnboundedLoop/DenialOfService family (in_class).
